key_highlights: Global bond selloff deepens as rising oil prices and inflation jitters push yields to multi-decade highs, triggering a broad risk-off move across equities.
The Dow briefly hit 50,000 on AI buzz but reversed sharply as surging yields and oil prices drove a selloff in chipmakers and tech stocks.
Cerebras IPO soared 90% on debut but slid the next day, highlighting extreme volatility in AI-related names.
Inflation fears are escalating: grocery prices jumped the most in nearly four years, producer prices are spiking, and the Conference Board sees prolonged inflation with the Fed holding steady.
Geopolitical risks from Trump's China trip (limited progress on Hormuz) and rising oil prices are compounding market stress.
Uranium and gold mining stocks are seeing significant moves on project updates and M&A, while silver slumps after a volatile week.
Insider buying activity (Vickers) suggests selective value opportunities amid the selloff.

sectors.trend: Sharp selloff in chipmakers as rising yields and inflation fears drive profit-taking after a scorching rally. Cerebras IPO volatility and Cisco's AI-driven job cuts underscore the sector's bifurcation.
sectors.implications: Near-term headwinds from higher discount rates and rotation out of high-duration assets. AI infrastructure spending remains robust but employment impact is minimal, raising questions about sustainability.

sectors.trend: Oil prices rise on geopolitical tensions (Hormuz) and strong demand, while uranium stocks rally on project restarts (Ur-Energy, NexGen) and AI data center power needs.
sectors.implications: Higher oil adds to inflation pressures, benefiting energy stocks but hurting consumers. Uranium is a structural growth play tied to nuclear power demand for AI data centers.

sectors.trend: Global bond selloff deepens, yields hit multi-decade highs, and G-7 discusses the rout. Corporate bonds are favored over sovereigns due to inflation risk.
sectors.implications: Rising yields pressure equity valuations and increase borrowing costs. Hedging flows (e.g., $200B from Morgan Stanley) are propping up currencies like the euro, but bond futures face rapid hedging overhaul risks.

sectors.trend: Gold mining stocks see selective interest (GoldMining, Gold Royalty), but silver slumps after volatile swings. Uranium and copper projects are gaining attention.
sectors.implications: Precious metals are mixed as real yields rise; uranium and copper offer thematic growth from energy transition and AI infrastructure.

sectors.trend: Consumers are cutting back on goods due to higher gas prices and grocery inflation. Subscription fatigue is emerging as a concern.
sectors.implications: Consumer discretionary spending may weaken, pressuring retail and e-commerce stocks. Defensive positioning in staples and discount retailers is warranted.

sectors.trend: AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon team up to eliminate coverage dead zones, signaling cooperative infrastructure investment.
sectors.implications: Positive for network quality and 5G adoption, but capital expenditure may weigh on near-term margins.

key_themes: Inflation resurgence and bond yield spike driving risk-off sentiment
Geopolitical uncertainty from Trump's China trip and Iran tensions
AI infrastructure boom but with employment and valuation concerns
Uranium and nuclear renaissance as a solution for AI data center power
Consumer strain from rising energy and food prices
Active vs passive investing debate amid market volatility
Insider buying signaling value opportunities in beaten-down sectors

risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce duration exposure, favor floating-rate bonds and TIPS. Increase allocation to value and energy sectors that benefit from inflation.
Hedge with oil and gold positions. Avoid direct exposure to regions with high geopolitical risk. Monitor diplomatic developments.
Rotate from high-multiple AI stocks to those with strong earnings and reasonable valuations. Consider selective profit-taking.
Underweight consumer discretionary, overweight consumer staples and discount retailers. Monitor jobless claims and retail sales.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Credit market stress (corporate bonds, private loans)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Favor investment-grade corporate bonds over high-yield. Monitor Goldman's risk transfer deals and emerging market debt.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Currency volatility (dollar strength, emerging market stress)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Hedge foreign currency exposure. Favor USD-denominated assets. Avoid emerging markets with high external debt.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy uranium stocks on pullbacks (UEC, NXE, DNN)
opportunities.rationale: Nuclear power demand for AI data centers and clean energy transition is structural. Federal clearance for NexGen and Ur-Energy restart signal strong industry momentum.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate investment-grade corporate bonds over sovereigns
opportunities.rationale: Inflation risk gives corporate bonds an edge as companies can pass on costs. Spreads are attractive relative to government bonds.

opportunities.recommendation: Selective insider buying signals: follow Vickers top picks
opportunities.rationale: Insider buying at current levels suggests undervaluation in certain names. Monitor daily Vickers reports for actionable ideas.
Rising oil prices and energy demand from AI data centers support energy stocks. Geothermal stocks offer long-term growth.
Dexcom's bet beyond diabetes into broader health monitoring could open large markets. Analysts are bullish post-earnings.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to high-duration tech and growth stocks
defensive_moves.rationale: Rising yields compress valuations for long-duration assets. Rotate into value, energy, and financials.
Cash provides optionality amid volatility. Short-duration bonds offer attractive yields with less price risk.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge with gold and gold miners
defensive_moves.rationale: Gold provides a hedge against inflation and geopolitical risk. Gold mining stocks offer leverage to gold prices.
Rising gas and grocery prices are squeezing consumers. Staples and discount retailers are more resilient.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid emerging market debt and equities
defensive_moves.rationale: Borrowing costs are soaring in emerging markets, and the global selloff is hitting them hard. Stick with developed markets.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Bearish with high volatility. Rising bond yields, inflation fears, and geopolitical tensions will keep risk assets under pressure. The Dow's brief touch of 50,000 may prove to be a near-term top. Expect further rotation out of growth into value and energy. The G-7 meeting and Fed commentary will be key.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: Cautiously optimistic. Inflation may moderate as supply chains adjust and the Fed holds steady. AI infrastructure spending and nuclear energy investment provide structural growth. Corporate bonds offer attractive yields. However, geopolitical risks and consumer strain could delay recovery.
key_catalysts: G-7 discussions on bond selloff and coordinated response
Fed meeting minutes and any shift in forward guidance
Oil price trajectory and Iran negotiations
US CPI and PCE data for May
Cerebras and other AI IPO aftermarket performance
Senate Banking Committee hearing on market structure bill
World Cup-related consumer spending and advertising (Roku)
Uranium project permits and construction progress
watch_list: Weekly jobless claims and retail sales data
